name: Sloth
summary: Brown-throated sloths are slow-moving tree mammals from Central American rainforests. They hang from branches, eat leaves, and spend most of their lives in the canopy.
metaTitle: Sloth Facts | Rainforest Habitat, Diet & Photos
metaDescription: Discover brown-throated sloth facts with photos, rainforest habitat, leafy diet, baby life, and slow movement. Learn where sloths live and why they move so slowly.
searchIntents: what is a baby sloth called
how do sloths hang upside down

lifespan.captivity: Up to 30 years in human care

speed: Very slow on ground and in trees
funFacts: Sloths move slowly because their leafy diet gives them very little energy.
Three-toed sloths have three claws on each front and hind foot.
Sloths spend most of their lives hanging upside down in trees.
Green algae sometimes grows in sloth fur and helps them blend in.
Sloths descend to the ground only about once a week.
Baby sloths cling to their mothers for months while learning to climb.

faq.question: What is a sloth?
faq.answer: A sloth is a slow-moving mammal that lives in trees and spends most of its time hanging from branches.

faq.question: Where do sloths live?
faq.answer: Brown-throated sloths live in tropical rainforests from Honduras through northern South America.

faq.question: What do sloths eat?
faq.answer: Sloths are herbivores that eat leaves, buds, and tender shoots from rainforest trees.

faq.question: Why are sloths so slow?
faq.answer: Leaves are low in calories, so sloths move slowly and rest often to save energy.

faq.question: How do sloths hang upside down?
faq.answer: Long curved claws hook over branches so sloths can hang without using much muscle strength.

faq.question: What is a baby sloth called?
faq.answer: A baby sloth is called an infant. Young sloths cling to their mothers while learning to climb and feed.

faq.question: Are sloths endangered?
faq.answer: Brown-throated sloths are listed as Least Concern, but habitat loss can harm local populations.
